findings.evidence: `rg -n "theorem algebraMap_int_mem_iff_dvd_of_liesOver|ncard_primesOver_eq_finrank_iff" code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/NumberField/SplitsCompletely.lean` shows the lemma at lines 46-52 has only `[CommRing B]`, while the surrounding file’s main public API at lines 67 and 85 is Galois number-field complete-splitting infrastructure.
findings.fix: Move it to an earlier generic ideal/ramification home, or keep it private/local to the multiquadratic splitting proof if no such home exists yet.
findings.issue: `algebraMap_int_mem_iff_dvd_of_liesOver` is a public generic lemma for any `[CommRing B]` and `Q.LiesOver (span {(p : ℤ)})`, not a complete-splitting or number-field result, so importing it drags in the Galois number-field splitting file for unrelated ideal membership facts.

summary: The private multiquadratic proof helpers are local to their only public theorem, and the added Mathlib import is tied to the new Galois-restriction code. The new public `LiesOver` membership lemma is generic commutative-ring infrastructure but is placed in a complete-splitting number-field file.
